description: The March 23 Movement (M23) is a Congolese rebel paramilitary group backed by Rwanda, based in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo. It was founded in 2012 by former members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) who mutinied against the government. M23 captured Goma in 2012 but withdrew after negotiations, and resumed operations in 2022. It is the principal member of the Congo River Alliance.

country_links.evidence_quote: Makenga sought to recruit additional fighters from the DRC, Rwanda, and Uganda. Although he received some assistance from sympathetic Rwandan officials who facilitated recruitment in Rwanda...
country_links.context: M23 recruits fighters from Rwanda and receives limited support from Rwandan officials.

country_links.evidence_quote: Recruitment also occurred in internally displaced persons camps throughout Ituri. Thousands of combatants received military training at facilities in Ituri, including AFC-M23 training centers located in Tchanzu, as well as in Uganda.
country_links.context: M23 uses Uganda for recruitment, training, and meetings to coordinate operations.
